documents.reveals: Alex Vance found dead after livestream abruptly cuts out.
documents.player_inference: The stream cutting out was not a technical glitch; it was the moment of death.
documents.document_justification: Provides the immediate hook and sets the public timeline of events.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: The 2.3M subscriber channel went dark at 2:47 AM, and building security found his body at 6:30 AM.
production_brief_writing.summary: Establish the setting, the victim's profile, and the timeline of discovery.

production_brief_newspaper.angle: Local tech celebrity found dead during viral charity event

quotes.gist: We are treating this as suspicious
He was always streaming; I cannot believe he is gone
Stream was raising thousands per hour; it went dark around 2:47 AM

production_brief_newspaper.planted_clue: Building management confirmed access control logged normal activity — contradicted by C1 entry/exit log

documents.image_brief: A newspaper front page with a photo of the exterior of the creator studio building.

image_requirements.brief: Building exterior, neon signs, police tape visible.

documents.reveals: The primary suspects and their relation to Alex.
documents.player_inference: Each suspect has a potential motive and access to the studio.
documents.document_justification: Mandatory document to introduce the cast of characters.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Suspects identified based on building access and recent interactions.
production_brief_writing.summary: Introduce the POIs clearly and concisely.

documents.image_brief: Mugshots or ID photos for all POIs.

documents.reveals: A shadow in the doorway right before the stream dies.
documents.player_inference: Someone was in the studio with Alex when the stream ended.
documents.document_justification: Visually establishes the critical moment of the crime.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Frame 4302: Unidentified shadow cast on the back wall near the studio entrance.
production_brief_writing.summary: Highlight the shadow in the final moments of the stream.

documents.reveals: Sam Miller DoorDash receipt shows a FuelBurger delivery at 2:51 AM — just 4 minutes after the stream died. The delivery address shows a partial building number matching the studio complex, but the specific entrance or unit is cut off on the receipt image.
documents.player_inference: This seems to provide an alibi for Sam at the time of the murder.
documents.document_justification: Trojan horse document. Appears to clear Sam, but actually implicates him later.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Order #4417 — FuelBurger — Delivered 2:51 AM — 1200 Tech Park [rest of address cut off in scan]
production_brief_writing.summary: Show the DoorDash receipt as a scanned image crop. The timestamp (2:51 AM) is clear but the delivery address is partially visible — the player sees the building number but not the specific entrance. This looks like an alibi for Sam (he was ordering food, not killing). The full address is revealed in C3.

documents.reveals: Ty posted a selfie geolocated near the studio.
documents.player_inference: Ty is lying about being home; he was physically close to the scene.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Just chilling outside the fortress. Hope Alex sees the dono! #VanceTech24
production_brief_writing.summary: Show Ty's obsession and physical proximity.

documents.reveals: Jordan claims no recent contact with Alex, citing a bitter lawsuit.
documents.player_inference: Jordan has a strong motive (money/revenge), but is he lying about contact?
documents.document_justification: Explores the business partner angle and introduces a contradiction.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: I haven't spoken to Alex since the lawyers took over. We were done.
production_brief_writing.summary: Establish Jordan's bitterness and establish his claim of no contact.

phases.label: Baseline
phases.tactic: Jordan is annoyed to be brought in, treating it like a waste of his valuable time.
phases.goal: Jordan is annoyed to be brought in, treating it like a waste of his valuable time.

phases.label: Pressure
phases.tactic: Detective presses on the lawsuit details and bad blood.
phases.goal: Detective presses on the lawsuit details and bad blood.

phases.label: The Lie
phases.tactic: Jordan insists he cut all ties and communication.
phases.goal: Jordan insists he cut all ties and communication.

phases.label: The Slip
phases.tactic: He mentions a specific detail about the studio setup that implies recent knowledge.
phases.goal: He mentions a specific detail about the studio setup that implies recent knowledge.

phases.label: Recovery
phases.tactic: He claims he read about the setup online.
phases.goal: He claims he read about the setup online.

production_brief_interview.the_lie: Jordan insists he cut all ties and communication.

the_slip.what: He mentions a specific detail about the studio setup that implies recent knowledge.

documents.reveals: Chloe dropped off the energy drinks and claims she bought them right before.
documents.player_inference: Chloe provided the method of poison delivery. Did she spike them?
documents.document_justification: Explores the girlfriend angle and the timeline of the drinks.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: I just grabbed the case on my way over. He needed his specific brand.
production_brief_writing.summary: Establish Chloe as the source of the drinks and her claim about the purchase time.
key_information_to_include: Chloe mentions buying energy drinks at 6:15 PM — hours before drop-off

phases.label: Baseline
phases.tactic: Chloe is visibly upset, crying intermittently.
phases.goal: Chloe is visibly upset, crying intermittently.

phases.label: Pressure
phases.tactic: Detective asks about the specific timeline of the drop-off.
phases.goal: Detective asks about the specific timeline of the drop-off.

phases.label: The Lie
phases.tactic: She insists she bought the drinks immediately before dropping them off.
phases.goal: She insists she bought the drinks immediately before dropping them off.

phases.label: The Slip
phases.tactic: She mentions checking her makeup in a specific store mirror that isn't near the studio.
phases.goal: She mentions checking her makeup in a specific store mirror that isn't near the studio.

phases.label: Recovery
phases.tactic: She claims she was just flustered and misremembered the route.
phases.goal: She claims she was just flustered and misremembered the route.

production_brief_interview.the_lie: She insists she bought the drinks immediately before dropping them off.

the_slip.what: Chloe says she grabbed the drinks on her way over, implying a quick spontaneous stop. But she accidentally mentions using the self-checkout at HEB, lane 7 — a specific detail that suggests a deliberate, unhurried shopping trip, not a quick grab. Combined with the 6:15 PM receipt timestamp, this proves she bought them hours before arriving.
the_slip.how: Detective asks casually where she picked up the drinks. Chloe says just on my way here, no big deal. Detective asks which store. She says HEB, self-checkout lane 7, the one near the pharmacy. The specificity betrays a planned trip, not an impulse buy.

documents.reveals: Sam relies heavily on his delivery alibi and feigns ignorance of the shadow.
documents.player_inference: Sam seems cleared, directing suspicion toward others.
documents.document_justification: Solidifies Sam's false alibi and establishes his deceptive demeanor.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: I was at home eating my late-night pad thai when the stream went down. Check the app.
production_brief_writing.summary: Lean into the DoorDash alibi to misdirect the player.

phases.label: Baseline
phases.tactic: Sam is cooperative, offering technical explanations for the stream failure.
phases.goal: Sam is cooperative, offering technical explanations for the stream failure.

phases.label: Pressure
phases.tactic: Detective asks where he was exactly.
phases.goal: Detective asks where he was exactly.

phases.label: The Lie
phases.tactic: He confidently states he was at his apartment.
phases.goal: He confidently states he was at his apartment.

phases.label: The Slip
phases.tactic: He perfectly recalls the timestamp of the outage without checking notes.
phases.goal: He perfectly recalls the timestamp of the outage without checking notes.

phases.label: Recovery
phases.tactic: He says it's his job to know the stream metrics.
phases.goal: He says it's his job to know the stream metrics.

production_brief_interview.the_lie: He confidently states he was at his apartment.

the_slip.what: Sam says the network switch in the control room was already rebooting when he arrived to check equipment — but the network logs show the switch was manually triggered at 2:47 AM, exactly when the stream died. How would Sam know it was rebooting unless he was already there?
the_slip.how: Detective asks Sam what he found when he went to check the equipment. Sam tries to minimize his involvement by saying everything was already resetting on its own. But rebooting implies he saw it mid-process, placing him in the control room during the critical window.

documents.reveals: Cause of death is a lethal dose of a prescription stimulant found in the stomach contents, mixed with an energy drink.
documents.player_inference: The murderer tampered with the drinks Chloe brought.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Toxicology indicates a massive overdose of dextroamphetamine, ingested alongside a high-caffeine beverage.
production_brief_writing.summary: Confirm the poison and its delivery method.

documents.image_brief: A body diagram showing no external trauma, focusing on internal toxicology.

documents.reveals: Tyler admits he was outside the studio building that night hoping to get noticed on stream, but insists he never went inside. He becomes defensive when asked about his escalating Discord messages.
documents.player_inference: Tyler had motive (obsessive fan behavior) and was physically near the scene, but did he actually enter the building?
documents.document_justification: Addresses the superfan angle. Tyler looks suspicious due to proximity and obsessive behavior, but building access logs in C1 prove he never entered.

production_brief_writing.summary: Full interrogation of Tyler Riggs, the superfan Discord moderator caught near the studio. Nervous, over-eager, then defensive about threats.
production_brief_writing.tone: nervous, overly cooperative, then defensive when cornered
production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: I was just sitting in my car in the parking lot, okay? I wanted to see if he would mention my name on stream. I never went inside.
key_information_to_include: Tyler was in the parking lot from approximately 1:00 AM to 3:30 AM
He saw Chloe leave around 10:45 PM
He noticed studio lights go dark at 2:47 AM
His Discord DMs escalated from fan messages to veiled threats
He claims the threats were just venting
production_brief_writing.voice_notes: Speaks fast, uses internet slang, apologizes constantly. When confronted about threats, switches to monosyllabic answers.

image_requirements.subject: POI-05
image_requirements.brief: 22yo male, hoodie, anxious, gaming headset around neck

phases.label: Rapport
phases.tactic: Let Tyler talk about his admiration for Alex

phases.label: Timeline
phases.tactic: Walk through his night hour by hour
phases.goal: Lock in claim of staying in car

phases.label: Pressure
phases.goal: Force him to explain escalating language

phases.label: The slip
phases.goal: Tyler mentions control room door knowledge he should not have

production_brief_interview.the_lie: Claims he never left his car and never entered the building

the_slip.what: Tyler mentions the door between the control room and studio — an interior detail not visible from outside or on the stream
the_slip.how: Over-describes building layout trying to be helpful, reveals interior knowledge

documents.reveals: Ty Riggs never badged into the building.
documents.player_inference: Ty was outside, but he couldn't have killed Alex because he couldn't get in.
documents.document_justification: Clears Ty Riggs and resolves his contradiction.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: No entry records found for T. Riggs or guest access codes during the specified timeframe.
production_brief_writing.summary: Prove Ty didn't enter the building.

documents.reveals: A 12-minute call between Alex and Jordan the day before.
documents.player_inference: Jordan lied about contact. However, an SMS snippet shows it was a final settlement agreement. Jordan was moving on, not planning murder.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: SMS: 'Fine. The lawyers handle the rest. I'm out.'
production_brief_writing.summary: Show the call happened, but the context clears Jordan of immediate murderous intent.

documents.reveals: DoorDash support inquiry email shows the FULL delivery details: order delivered to 1200 Tech Park Dr, Unit B — Control Room Side Entrance. This is not Sam apartment. This is the studio control room, proving Sam was physically in the control room at 2:51 AM, 4 minutes after the stream died and Alex was killed.
documents.player_inference: Sam was AT THE STUDIO in the control room at 2:51 AM, not at his apartment.
documents.document_justification: The pivot document. Destroys Sam's alibi using the Trojan horse evidence.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Delivery address on file: 1200 Tech Park Dr, Unit B — Control Room Side Entrance. Customer note: leave at door, do not ring.
production_brief_writing.summary: Email exchange between DoorDash support and APD showing full order details. The key revelation: the delivery address specifies Control Room Side Entrance. This is the pivot document that breaks Sam alibi — he was not at home ordering food, he was in the control room next to the studio where Alex died.

documents.reveals: The control room connects directly to the main studio.
documents.player_inference: Sam could poison the drink, cut the stream from the control room, and step into the studio (casting the shadow) without ever entering the main hall.
documents.document_justification: Provides the spatial reality needed to understand how the crime was committed.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Annotated detail: Connecting door between Control Room and Main Studio.
production_brief_writing.summary: Show the physical connection that makes Sam's method possible.

documents.image_brief: A floor plan showing the main studio, the adjacent control room, the side entrance, and the connecting door.

image_requirements.brief: Clear labels for all relevant rooms and doors.

documents.reveals: Chloe Bennett is cleared. Grocery receipt proves she bought drinks hours earlier, CCTV confirms she left the building at 10:45 PM — over 4 hours before the murder. Her prescribed medication is unrelated to the case.
documents.player_inference: She provided the drinks, but didn't poison them.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: No charges filed against Ms. Bennett; timeline of purchase clears her of direct tampering prior to delivery.

documents.reveals: Jordan Hayes is cleared. Phone records confirm the 12-minute call was a settlement negotiation through lawyers. His keycard was deactivated 6 months ago, and building access logs show no entry attempts.
documents.player_inference: He had motive, but was finalizing a settlement, not planning a murder.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Investigation into Mr. Hayes concludes civil litigation was the extent of his ongoing conflict with the deceased.

documents.reveals: Tyler 'Ty' Riggs is cleared. Despite being in the parking lot all night and sending threatening Discord DMs, building access logs confirm he never entered. His knowledge of the control room door came from an old studio tour video.
documents.player_inference: He was creepy, but couldn't get inside.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Mr. Riggs' physical presence outside the building is noted, but lack of entry confirms his non-involvement in the primary incident.

documents.reveals: Sam is arrested for the murder of Alex Vance.
documents.player_inference: Sam's alibi was broken, placing him at the scene with the means, motive, and opportunity.
documents.document_justification: Mandatory resolution doc naming the culprit.

production_brief_writing.key_mandatory_line: Warrant authorized for the arrest of Sam Miller based on evidence placing him in the control room adjacent to the victim at the time of death.
production_brief_writing.summary: Arrest Sam and summarize the evidence against him.
